title: P-first symplectic Euler has determinant-one linear update

source.notes: Determinant-preservation seed for the p-first symplectic Euler linear update. It complements the orbit-invariant records with an area-preservation theorem shape.

informal.statement: The p-first symplectic Euler update pNext = p - dt omega^2 q and qNext = q + dt pNext has linear coefficient matrix [[1 - dt^2 omega^2, dt], [-dt omega^2, 1]] in the state variables (q, p). The determinant of this 2-by-2 matrix is 1.
informal.latex: For the p-first symplectic Euler update \(p_+=p-\Delta t\,\omega^2q\), \(q_+=q+\Delta t\,p_+\), the induced linear map on \((q,p)\) has coefficient matrix \(\begin{pmatrix}1-(\Delta t)^2\omega^2 & \Delta t\\ -\Delta t\,\omega^2 & 1\end{pmatrix}\). Its determinant is \(1\).
assumptions: omega and dt are real numbers
the p-first symplectic Euler update is written as a linear map in q and p
the 2-by-2 determinant is represented as a * d - b * c
informal.expected_conclusion: The determinant of the p-first symplectic Euler coefficient matrix equals one.

declarations.name: det2
declarations.statement: def det2 (a b c d : ℝ) : ℝ := a * d - b * c

declarations.name: symplecticEulerPNext_linear
declarations.statement: theorem symplecticEulerPNext_linear (omega dt q p : ℝ) : symplecticEulerPNext omega dt q p = (-dt * omega ^ 2) * q + 1 * p

declarations.name: symplecticEulerQNext_linear
declarations.statement: theorem symplecticEulerQNext_linear (omega dt q p : ℝ) : symplecticEulerQNext omega dt q p = (1 - dt ^ 2 * omega ^ 2) * q + dt * p

declarations.name: symplecticEulerLinear_det_eq_one
declarations.statement: theorem symplecticEulerLinear_det_eq_one (omega dt : ℝ) : det2 (1 - dt ^ 2 * omega ^ 2) dt (-dt * omega ^ 2) 1 = 1

tasks.input: Translate the determinant-one statement for the p-first symplectic Euler coefficient matrix into Lean 4 using det2.
tasks.target: theorem symplecticEulerLinear_det_eq_one (omega dt : ℝ) : det2 (1 - dt ^ 2 * omega ^ 2) dt (-dt * omega ^ 2) 1 = 1

tasks.input: Given the det2 definition and determinant theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof using unfold det2 and ring.

tasks.input: Identify which LeanNumerics linear-coefficient lemmas justify the coefficient matrix used in the determinant theorem.
